title: Not for the Money: How Work Orientations Shape Responses to Performance Pay in Seasonal Labor
data_source: GROW program: 55,825 worker-month observations, 13,989 unique workers, 6 facilities, 12 months (Apr 2021-Mar 2022), 77 interviews

categories.framing: Work orientations as filter for performance pay response: career-oriented, flexibility-seekers, income-targeters respond differently to incentives
categories.mechanism: Workers' pre-existing orientation toward work determines whether performance pay motivates or is ignored. Misfit between incentive design and orientation can paradoxically motivate.
categories.evidence: 55,825 worker-month observations, 13,989 unique workers, performance incentive data showing 0.21 vs 0.12 response rates
categories.claims: Three work orientations predict differential response to performance pay. Career-oriented workers respond most strongly despite money not being primary motivation.
categories.boundaries: Seasonal warehouse labor, single company (GROW program)
categories.architecture: Standard mixed-methods: Intro, Theory, Methods, Findings, Discussion

events.summary: Initial frame as mixed-methods orientation paper
events.detail: Paper built around the puzzle that seasonal warehouse workers respond very differently to identical performance pay systems. Core insight from 77 interviews: workers have distinct orientations (career, flexibility, income) that filter their response to pay incentives. Quantitative data from 55,825 worker-month observations confirms differential response patterns.
events.trigger: Dormant data from GROW warehouse study; theory-forge pipeline applied

categories.validation: ZIP CRITIQUE: Sent to external model for adversarial review

events.summary: Zip critique: sent to external model
events.detail: Packaged manuscript with data and analysis code for external adversarial review. Included 77 interview transcripts, quantitative data, and analysis scripts.

events.version_name: External Review: Theoretically Strong, Structurally Overloaded

categories.claims: Reviewer notes causal interpretation stronger than research design supports
categories.boundaries: Work orientations empirically thin — need clearer operationalization
categories.validation: External review (8,300+ words): 'substantively strong and interesting' but overloaded theoretical stack, three major issues identified

events.summary: External review: strong substance, structural issues
events.detail: Adversarial review returned verdict: substantively strong and interesting, but (1) overloaded theoretical stack trying to do too much, (2) causal interpretation stronger than design supports, (3) work orientations concept empirically thin. Recommendation: tighten scope, strengthen orientation operationalization.

categories.evidence: CRITICAL FINDING: Fourth orientation type discovered — income maximizers distinct from income-targeters
categories.validation: Adversarial audit of 77 interview transcripts. 8 major claims assessed. Income maximizers finding.
categories.replication: Living Paper infrastructure created: 33 claims, 91 evidence links

events.summary: Claims audit discovers fourth orientation type
events.detail: Comprehensive audit of 77 interview transcripts. Key finding: income maximizers are distinct from income-targeters — they actively seek maximum hours/overtime rather than targeting a specific income level. This challenges the three-orientation framework. Also created Living Paper infrastructure with 33 claims and 91 evidence links.

categories.replication: Validation package created for external sharing

events.detail: Created validation package for potential external sharing. Overleaf export also created.
Commit 164da3e: 'Initial commit: warehouse automation papers (manuscripts only)'. Project formally tracked in git.

categories.architecture: Major structural revision based on external review feedback

events.detail: Created STRUCTURAL_REVISION_DRAFT.md addressing the three major issues from external review: tightened theoretical stack, better operationalization of orientations, more cautious causal language.

events.version_name: Repoint Drafts: Title Shift to 'Not for the Money'

categories.framing: Reframed: 'When Misfit Motivates' → 'Not for the Money'. Shifted emphasis from misfit paradox to orientation-filtered incentive response.

events.detail: Created REPOINT_DRAFT.md and REPOINT_DRAFT_v2.md. Renamed paper from 'When Misfit Motivates' to 'Not for the Money'. The new title better captures the core puzzle: why do workers who aren't motivated by money respond most strongly to performance pay?
Paper submitted to Organization Science. Cover letter and paper body finalized. Submission documents include .docx versions, proof PDF, and cover letter.
events.trigger: Manuscript deemed ready after structural revisions

categories.framing: Sharpened: Added boundary condition (signaling theory scope), resolved conflicting theoretical framing between flexibility and career orientations
categories.mechanism: Strengthened: Tightened signaling definition, added Lazear engagement, anchored citations to theory claims
categories.evidence: Cleaned: 5 unused bib entries removed, 1 new citation added (Connelly 2011), 2 date mismatches flagged, 2 entries restored
categories.claims: Refined: Null hypothesis sharpened, boundary conditions made explicit
categories.boundaries: Added: Signaling theory scope condition in theory section
categories.validation: Full theory-forge evaluation: 8 evaluations across 6 background agents (Zuckerman, citations, limitations, genre, contribution, quality). Hard gates D and F passed.
categories.automation_added: /review-diff tool built: annotated HTML diff viewer for reviewing AI-created changes
categories.workflow_refinement: Diff review workflow established: generate diffs against Overleaf version, annotate with rationale, review in HTML viewer

events.summary: Theory-forge full evaluation and 10 revision tasks
events.detail: Ran complete theory-forge evaluation pipeline: 8 evaluations across 6 parallel agents. Generated synthesis with 10 specific revision tasks. All tasks executed: (1) restructured theory section boundary, (2) sharpened signaling definition, (3) added null hypothesis, (4) added boundary conditions, (5) replaced genre words, (6) anchored citations, (7) engaged Lazear, (8) resolved framing ambiguity, (9) cleaned bibliography, (10) fixed citation dates. Committed as 2aec94d. Also built /review-diff tool for theory-forge.
events.trigger: Post-submission quality check; theory-forge evaluation pipeline matured
